function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND c.status = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cc.closeddate - cc.createddate))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND (c.status = 'Closed' OR c.status LIKE 'Closed%')
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
                           TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
    AND agent_id NOT LIKE '#%'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != ''
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
)
SELECT 
    cc.agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM ClosedCases cc
JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
GROUP BY cc.agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
        AND agent_id != 'None'
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
)
SELECT 
    asts.agent_id,
    asts.case_count,
    ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ), 2) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases ntc
JOIN AgentStats asts ON ntc.agent_id = asts.agent_id
GROUP BY asts.agent_id, asts.case_count
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Just get all closed cases in the past 4 months and count them
SELECT 
    c.id as case_id,
    TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
    c.status,
    CASE 
        WHEN c.closeddate != 'None' AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL THEN 'has_closed'
        ELSE 'no_closed'
    END as closed_status_check
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
    AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND c.closeddate != 'None'
LIMIT 20
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != ''
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
             TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF'))
        ) / 3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count,
        AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT agent_id
FROM AgentStats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

    TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,

FROM "Case" c

"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
SELECT TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
       COUNT(*) as total_cases,
       MIN(c.createddate) as earliest,
       MAX(c.createddate) as latest
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
  AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
  AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
  AND c.closeddate != 'None'
GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid)
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY total_cases DESC
LIMIT 20
"}

TOOLS (system will execute):

- list_db: list tables or collections for a given database.
  Required args: {"db_name": "<logical_db_name>"}
- execute_python: run Python code.
  Required args: {"code": "<python_code>"}
- return_answer: finish and return the final answer (plain text).
  Required args: {"answer": "<final plain-text answer>"}

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. After each tool call, its result will be stored in a storage under a key named after the tool call id (you will be told the key name). The next message will include both the result (or a preview if it's large) and the storage key name.
2. Inside execute_python code you may read storage entries using the provided key names, e.g., if the tool call id is 'functions.tool:id', you can access its result via `locals()['var_functions.tool:id']` in your code.
3. You cannot modify or reassign those storage-provided variables; you may read them and create new variables as needed.
4. If a tool result is large, the next message will include a preview (first 10000 characters) and the storage entry will be the .json file path (a string) where the full result is stored. To access the full result, your execute_python code must open and read that .json file.

KEY RULES (must follow exactly):
1. Always use tool calls. Do not output plain text, explanations, or reasoning.
2. Include all required arguments for the tool you call.
3. For query_db, always specify db_name and query. Refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION for db_name and query format.
4. For PostgreSQL, wrap mixed-case or uppercase column names in double quotes.
5. For list_db, refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION to specify db_name.
6. Use execute_python for data processing as needed. 
7. When using execute_python, your code will be quoted by triple double-quotes and passed as a string to `exec(...)` for execution in a Python 3.12 environment with only pandas and pyarrow installed. So you must ensure your code is compatible with this execution method. For exampe, do not use triple double-quotes in your code, as they may interfere with parsing. Do not use non-built-in or non-installed packages.
8. When using execute_python, your code must print the result at the end exactly as shown in the PRINT FORMAT section below. The printed result must be a string that can be successfully parsed by json.loads() without errors.

PRINT FORMAT (must match exactly):
----BEGIN PRINT FORMAT----
print("__RESULT__:")
print(your_json_serializable_string_here)
----END PRINT FORMAT----

For simple types (int, float, str, bool, None), you may use json.dumps() to produce a valid JSON string.
For complex or non-JSON-serializable types, you must convert them into JSON-compatible forms before printing.
For lists or dictionaries, you must ensure that all nested elements are also converted into JSON-serializable types.
9. Return the final answer only via a single return_answer tool call. Do not include extra text, explanation, or formatting.

EXAMPLES:
- query_db:
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "SELECT * FROM some_table LIMIT some_limit;"}} # for SQL databases

df1 = pd.DataFrame(rl1)

If you cannot proceed, call return_answer with a short explanatory message.

Do not output explanations, reasoning, or any natural language outside of the required tool calls.

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

HINTS: 
- ~25% of ID-like fields may include a leading # (e.g., #001Wt00000PFj4zIAD).
- ~20% of text fields may contain trailing whitespace (e.g., "Company Name ").
- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.
- Corruption handling is needed for reliable joins
- Domain-specific CRM knowledge is required

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND c.status = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cc.closeddate - cc.createddate))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND (c.status = 'Closed' OR c.status LIKE 'Closed%')
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
                           TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
    AND agent_id NOT LIKE '#%'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != ''
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
)
SELECT 
    cc.agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM ClosedCases cc
JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
GROUP BY cc.agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
        AND agent_id != 'None'
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
)
SELECT 
    asts.agent_id,
    asts.case_count,
    ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ), 2) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases ntc
JOIN AgentStats asts ON ntc.agent_id = asts.agent_id
GROUP BY asts.agent_id, asts.case_count
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Just get all closed cases in the past 4 months and count them
SELECT 
    c.id as case_id,
    TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
    c.status,
    CASE 
        WHEN c.closeddate != 'None' AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL THEN 'has_closed'
        ELSE 'no_closed'
    END as closed_status_check
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
    AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND c.closeddate != 'None'
LIMIT 20
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != ''
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
             TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF'))
        ) / 3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count,
        AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT agent_id
FROM AgentStats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

SELECT 
    c.id,
    c.createddate,
    c.closeddate,
    TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
    c.status
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.status IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY c.createddate DESC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as final_agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
),
CaseAssignmentCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c as case_id,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
        MAX(ch.newvalue__c) as final_owner_from_history
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.final_agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
             TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF'))
        ) / 3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN CaseAssignmentCounts cac ON cc.case_id = cac.case_id
    WHERE cac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentCaseCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        final_agent_id as agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE final_agent_id IS NOT NULL
        AND final_agent_id != 'None'
    GROUP BY final_agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
),
AgentAvgHandleTime AS (
    SELECT 
        ntc.final_agent_id as agent_id,
        AVG(ntc.handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
    FROM NonTransferredCases ntc
    JOIN AgentCaseCounts acc ON ntc.final_agent_id = acc.agent_id
    GROUP BY ntc.final_agent_id
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    ROUND(avg_handle_time_hours::numeric, 2) as avg_hours
FROM AgentAvgHandleTime
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 10
"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

HINTS: 
- ~25% of ID-like fields may include a leading # (e.g., #001Wt00000PFj4zIAD).
- ~20% of text fields may contain trailing whitespace (e.g., "Company Name ").
- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.
- Corruption handling is needed for reliable joins
- Domain-specific CRM knowledge is required

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND c.status = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cc.closeddate - cc.createddate))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 10
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND (c.status = 'Closed' OR c.status LIKE 'Closed%')
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
                           TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
    AND agent_id NOT LIKE '#%'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != ''
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
)
SELECT 
    cc.agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM ClosedCases cc
JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
GROUP BY cc.agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
        AND agent_id != 'None'
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
)
SELECT 
    asts.agent_id,
    asts.case_count,
    ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ), 2) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases ntc
JOIN AgentStats asts ON ntc.agent_id = asts.agent_id
GROUP BY asts.agent_id, asts.case_count
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Just get all closed cases in the past 4 months and count them
SELECT 
    c.id as case_id,
    TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
    c.status,
    CASE 
        WHEN c.closeddate != 'None' AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL THEN 'has_closed'
        ELSE 'no_closed'
    END as closed_status_check
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
    AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND c.closeddate != 'None'
LIMIT 20
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != ''
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
             TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF'))
        ) / 3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count,
        AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT agent_id
FROM AgentStats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

    TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
    c.status
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.status IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY c.createddate DESC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as final_agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
),
CaseAssignmentCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c as case_id,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
        MAX(ch.newvalue__c) as final_owner_from_history
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.final_agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
             TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF'))
        ) / 3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN CaseAssignmentCounts cac ON cc.case_id = cac.case_id
    WHERE cac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentCaseCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        final_agent_id as agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE final_agent_id IS NOT NULL
        AND final_agent_id != 'None'
    GROUP BY final_agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
),
AgentAvgHandleTime AS (
    SELECT 
        ntc.final_agent_id as agent_id,
        AVG(ntc.handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
    FROM NonTransferredCases ntc
    JOIN AgentCaseCounts acc ON ntc.final_agent_id = acc.agent_id
    GROUP BY ntc.final_agent_id
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    ROUND(avg_handle_time_hours::numeric, 2) as avg_hours
FROM AgentAvgHandleTime
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 10
"}

SELECT TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
       COUNT(*) as total_cases,
       MIN(c.createddate) as earliest,
       MAX(c.createddate) as latest
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
  AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
  AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
  AND c.closeddate != 'None'
GROUP BY TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid)
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY total_cases DESC
LIMIT 20
"}

  Required args: {"db_name": "<logical_db_name>", "query": "<SQL or Mongo query>"}
- list_db: list tables or collections for a given database.
  Required args: {"db_name": "<logical_db_name>"}
- execute_python: run Python code.
  Required args: {"code": "<python_code>"}
- return_answer: finish and return the final answer (plain text).
  Required args: {"answer": "<final plain-text answer>"}

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. After each tool call, its result will be stored in a storage under a key named after the tool call id (you will be told the key name). The next message will include both the result (or a preview if it's large) and the storage key name.
2. Inside execute_python code you may read storage entries using the provided key names, e.g., if the tool call id is 'functions.tool:id', you can access its result via `locals()['var_functions.tool:id']` in your code.
3. You cannot modify or reassign those storage-provided variables; you may read them and create new variables as needed.
4. If a tool result is large, the next message will include a preview (first 10000 characters) and the storage entry will be the .json file path (a string) where the full result is stored. To access the full result, your execute_python code must open and read that .json file.

KEY RULES (must follow exactly):
1. Always use tool calls. Do not output plain text, explanations, or reasoning.
2. Include all required arguments for the tool you call.
3. For query_db, always specify db_name and query. Refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION for db_name and query format.
4. For PostgreSQL, wrap mixed-case or uppercase column names in double quotes.
5. For list_db, refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION to specify db_name.
6. Use execute_python for data processing as needed. 
7. When using execute_python, your code will be quoted by triple double-quotes and passed as a string to `exec(...)` for execution in a Python 3.12 environment with only pandas and pyarrow installed. So you must ensure your code is compatible with this execution method. For exampe, do not use triple double-quotes in your code, as they may interfere with parsing. Do not use non-built-in or non-installed packages.
8. When using execute_python, your code must print the result at the end exactly as shown in the PRINT FORMAT section below. The printed result must be a string that can be successfully parsed by json.loads() without errors.

PRINT FORMAT (must match exactly):
----BEGIN PRINT FORMAT----
print("__RESULT__:")
print(your_json_serializable_string_here)
----END PRINT FORMAT----

For simple types (int, float, str, bool, None), you may use json.dumps() to produce a valid JSON string.
For complex or non-JSON-serializable types, you must convert them into JSON-compatible forms before printing.
For lists or dictionaries, you must ensure that all nested elements are also converted into JSON-serializable types.
9. Return the final answer only via a single return_answer tool call. Do not include extra text, explanation, or formatting.

EXAMPLES:
- query_db:
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "SELECT * FROM some_table LIMIT some_limit;"}} # for SQL databases
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "{"collection": "some_collection", "filter": {some_filter}, "projection": {some_projection}, "limit": some_limit}"}} # for MongoDB databases

- list_db:
{"tool": "list_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name"}}

- execute_python:
{"tool": "execute_python", "args": {"code": "import pandas as pd
# rl1 and rl2 are the keys of two JSON-serializable record lists in storage
df1 = pd.DataFrame(rl1)
df2 = pd.DataFrame(rl2)
result = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='id').head(10).to_json(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)"}}

- return_answer:
{"tool": "return_answer", "args": {"answer": "…final plain-text answer…"}}

If you cannot proceed, call return_answer with a short explanatory message.

Do not output explanations, reasoning, or any natural language outside of the required tool calls.

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

HINTS: 
- ~25% of ID-like fields may include a leading # (e.g., #001Wt00000PFj4zIAD).
- ~20% of text fields may contain trailing whitespace (e.g., "Company Name ").
- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.
- Corruption handling is needed for reliable joins
- Domain-specific CRM knowledge is required

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND c.status = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cc.closeddate - cc.createddate))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND (c.status = 'Closed' OR c.status LIKE 'Closed%')
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
                           TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
    AND agent_id NOT LIKE '#%'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

-- Get all closed cases in the past 4 months with proper date handling
SELECT 
    c.id as case_id,
    TRIM(c.ownerid) as agent_id,
    c.createddate,
    c.closeddate
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
    AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND c.closeddate != 'None'
    AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
    AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
    AND TRIM(c.ownerid) != ''
    AND TRIM(c.ownerid) != 'None'
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != ''
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
)
SELECT 
    cc.agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM ClosedCases cc
JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
GROUP BY cc.agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
        AND agent_id != 'None'
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
)
SELECT 
    asts.agent_id,
    asts.case_count,
    ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ), 2) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases ntc
JOIN AgentStats asts ON ntc.agent_id = asts.agent_id
GROUP BY asts.agent_id, asts.case_count
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Just get all closed cases in the past 4 months and count them
SELECT 
    c.id as case_id,
    TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
    c.status,
    CASE 
        WHEN c.closeddate != 'None' AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL THEN 'has_closed'
        ELSE 'no_closed'
    END as closed_status_check
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
    AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND c.closeddate != 'None'
LIMIT 20
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != ''
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
             TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF'))
        ) / 3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count,
        AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT agent_id
FROM AgentStats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as final_agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
),
CaseAssignmentCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c as case_id,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count,
        MAX(ch.newvalue__c) as final_owner_from_history
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.final_agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
             TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF'))
        ) / 3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN CaseAssignmentCounts cac ON cc.case_id = cac.case_id
    WHERE cac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentCaseCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        final_agent_id as agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE final_agent_id IS NOT NULL
        AND final_agent_id != 'None'
    GROUP BY final_agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
),
AgentAvgHandleTime AS (
    SELECT 
        ntc.final_agent_id as agent_id,
        AVG(ntc.handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
    FROM NonTransferredCases ntc
    JOIN AgentCaseCounts acc ON ntc.final_agent_id = acc.agent_id
    GROUP BY ntc.final_agent_id
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    ROUND(avg_handle_time_hours::numeric, 2) as avg_hours
FROM AgentAvgHandleTime
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 10
"}

  AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
  AND c.closeddate != 'None'

ORDER BY total_cases DESC
LIMIT 20
"}

message.reasoning:
The user asks: "In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent."

From the problem definition:
- Handle time = time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- "agents managed/queries x cases" applies to both first assigned and transferred agents.
- When computing handle time, we do NOT compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases NOT transferred, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment'. For cases that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

So the logic is:
1. Get all cases created in the past four months (from approximately 2023-05-02 to 2023-09-02, given today is 2023-09-02).
2. Among these, find cases that are closed.
3. Filter out cases that have been transferred (i.e., cases with more than one 'Owner Assignment' in casehistory__c).
4. For the remaining cases (non-transferred), calculate handle time for each case.
5. Group by agent (ownerid).
6. Filter agents who have processed more than one case.
7. Find the agent with the lowest average handle time.
8. Return only that agent's Id.

Based on the queries I ran:
- var_functions.query_db_5: Sample of Case table data, showing closeddate can be "None" string and there are trailing spaces in status.
- var_functions.query_db_8: Shows createddate and closeddate are text fields, not timestamps.
- var_functions.query_db_18: Count of closed cases in range, got 21 total.
- var_functions.query_db_20: Sample of closed cases in the date range.
- var_functions.query_db_26: Sample of cases to check data quality.
- var_functions.query_db_28: Full result set of closed cases (stored in file).
- var_functions.query_db_36: Owner Assignment history records in date range.
- var_functions.query_db_40: Sample of cases with proper date range filter.
- var_functions.query_db_42: Closed cases in date range with cleaned agent_id.
- var_functions.query_db_44: My main query that returned empty.
- var_functions.query_db_46: Agent case counts - also empty.
- var_functions.query_db_48: Status distribution showing 33 Closed cases total.
- var_functions.query_db_50: All closed cases sorted by date.

Looking at var_functions.query_db_50, I can see there are cases from 2023-05-02 to 2023-09-30, but we're only interested in the past 4 months from 2023-09-02, which means up to 2023-05-02. So the range is correct.

The issue with my previous queries is likely:
1. Date comparison as strings might not work correctly
2. Handling of corrupted ID fields with leading #
3. Data quality issues

Let me try a different approach. I'll fetch all the necessary data and do the processing in Python to have better control over data cleaning and calculations.

First, I need to:
1. Get all cases in the date range that are closed
2. Get all owner assignment history for those cases
3. Process in Python to filter, calculate handle times, and find the agent with lowest average handle time

Let me fetch:
- All closed cases from 2023-05-02 to 2023-09-02
- All owner assignment history for cases in that range

Then process in Python.

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

HINTS: 
- ~25% of ID-like fields may include a leading # (e.g., #001Wt00000PFj4zIAD).
- ~20% of text fields may contain trailing whitespace (e.g., "Company Name ").
- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.
- Corruption handling is needed for reliable joins
- Domain-specific CRM knowledge is required

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND c.status = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cc.closeddate - cc.createddate))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 10
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id, 
        c.ownerid as agent_id, 
        c.createddate, 
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND (c.status = 'Closed' OR c.status LIKE 'Closed%')
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
                           TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
)
SELECT 
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases
WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    AND agent_id != ''
    AND agent_id != 'None'
    AND agent_id NOT LIKE '#%'
GROUP BY agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != ''
        AND TRIM(c.ownerid, '#') != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
)
SELECT 
    cc.agent_id,
    COUNT(*) as case_count,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM ClosedCases cc
JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
GROUP BY cc.agent_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
        AND agent_id != 'None'
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
)
SELECT 
    asts.agent_id,
    asts.case_count,
    ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
        (TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
         TO_TIMESTAMP(ntc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF')))/3600.0
    ), 2) as avg_handle_time_hours
FROM NonTransferredCases ntc
JOIN AgentStats asts ON ntc.agent_id = asts.agent_id
GROUP BY asts.agent_id, asts.case_count
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 5
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
-- Just get all closed cases in the past 4 months and count them
SELECT 
    c.id as case_id,
    TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
    c.status,
    CASE 
        WHEN c.closeddate != 'None' AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL THEN 'has_closed'
        ELSE 'no_closed'
    END as closed_status_check
FROM "Case" c
WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
    AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
    AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
    AND c.closeddate != 'None'
LIMIT 20
"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name": "support", "query": "
WITH ClosedCases AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id as case_id,
        TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) as agent_id,
        c.createddate,
        c.closeddate
    FROM "Case" c
    WHERE c.createddate >= '2023-05-02'
        AND c.createddate <= '2023-09-02'
        AND c.closeddate IS NOT NULL 
        AND c.closeddate != 'None'
        AND TRIM(c.status) = 'Closed'
        AND c.ownerid IS NOT NULL
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != ''
        AND TRIM(BOTH '#' FROM c.ownerid) != 'None'
),
OwnerAssignmentCount AS (
    SELECT 
        ch.caseid__c,
        COUNT(*) as assignment_count
    FROM casehistory__c ch
    WHERE ch.field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
        AND ch.caseid__c IN (SELECT case_id FROM ClosedCases)
    GROUP BY ch.caseid__c
),
NonTransferredCases AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.case_id,
        cc.agent_id,
        cc.createddate,
        cc.closeddate,
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            (TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.closeddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF') - 
             TO_TIMESTAMP(cc.createddate, 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF'))
        ) / 3600.0 as handle_time_hours
    FROM ClosedCases cc
    JOIN OwnerAssignmentCount oac ON cc.case_id = oac.caseid__c
    WHERE oac.assignment_count = 1
),
AgentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        agent_id,
        COUNT(*) as case_count,
        AVG(handle_time_hours) as avg_handle_time_hours
    FROM NonTransferredCases
    WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY agent_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT agent_id
FROM AgentStats
ORDER BY avg_handle_time_hours ASC
LIMIT 1
"}
